personal.name: Agrim Sigdel
personal.summary: Versatile Full-Stack Developer with a strong foundation in modern AI, engineering production-ready applications using React/TypeScript and Python/Django. Adept at building scalable REST APIs, optimizing relational databases, and implementing clean architecture across web, mobile, and open-source platforms. I bridge the gap between complex AI models and user-centric applications.
personal.shortSummary: Full-Stack Developer & AI Researcher building production-ready applications with React, TypeScript, and Python/Django - and shipping open-source tools.

education.degree: BSc. Computer Science and Information Technology (CSIT)
education.school: Prime College, Tribhuvan University | Kathmandu, Nepal

description: Engineer a high-performance mobile application using React Native and TypeScript, with strict adherence to component state-management best practices.
Refactor and optimize backend REST APIs for seamless compatibility, efficient serialization, and low-latency payloads between server and client.
Debug and resolve full-stack integration bottlenecks, improving database query handling and streamlining endpoints for real-time features.
Spearheaded full-stack development, optimization, and deployment for Feriaau and its multi-tenant CRM platform.
Owned the complete lifecycle - modular Python/Django backend services, robust REST APIs, and dynamic React.js/TypeScript interfaces.
Configured secure production servers and automated CI/CD pipelines, ensuring high availability and seamless code delivery.
Optimized PostgreSQL/MySQL schemas and queries to maximize application speed and multi-tenant isolation.
Led creative direction and brand guidelines for 625+ attendees, coordinating event planning and execution.
Managed cross-functional teams for visual and marketing materials, practicing agile task delegation.
Designed, built, and shipped a range of client projects end-to-end - from responsive marketing websites to full-stack web applications.
Worked directly with clients to scope requirements, translate them into clean designs, and deliver production-ready code on schedule.
Handled the full delivery pipeline including frontend, backend, deployment, and post-launch maintenance across varied tech stacks.

research.citation: Sigdel, A., Thapa, S., Thapa Magar, B., & Gajurel, R.K. "Cascaded AI Task Decomposition: A Generalizable Framework for Multi-Stage Computer Vision Pipelines Validated on Automated Number Plate Recognition for Nepali License Plates."
research.title: Cascaded AI Task Decomposition (CATD)
research.summary: A generalizable design pattern for multi-stage computer vision pipelines, validated on bi-scriptual Nepali license-plate recognition.
highlights: Formalized the CATD framework as a generalizable design pattern for multi-stage CV pipelines with applications beyond ANPR.
Achieved 97.9% mAP50 plate localization and 98.9% mAP50 character recognition using custom-trained YOLOv11n and RT-DETR Base on 61-class bi-scriptual Nepali plates.
Built a two-dimensional cross-domain training strategy (1,380 Nepali + 1,127 Indian plates) enabling compositional zero-shot transfer (+24.5 pp improvement).
Engineered a three-stage cascaded pipeline (YOLOv8n → YOLOv11n → RT-DETR) reaching 90.4% end-to-end precision with 61%–73% compute reduction via intelligent vehicle gating.
Integrated the system as the computer-vision backbone of a smart-parking platform handling automated billing and real-time traffic analytics.

projects.title: Feriaau
projects.description: Lead full-stack engineer for Feriaau - a production web platform built on a modular Python/Django backend with a dynamic React.js/TypeScript frontend, deployed on secure servers with automated CI/CD.
projects.outcome: Shipped and maintained in production with high availability and a streamlined release pipeline.

caseStudy.tagline: A production web platform on a modular Django backend and a dynamic React/TypeScript frontend.
highlights: Shipped and maintained in production with high availability
Modular Django backend with a decoupled React/TypeScript SPA
Automated CI/CD release pipeline

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Feriaau is a production web platform I lead as full-stack engineer. It pairs a modular Python/Django backend with a dynamic React.js/TypeScript frontend, deployed on secured servers behind an automated release pipeline. My focus here is the engineering: a clean, decoupled architecture that stays maintainable as the product grows.

sections.heading: My Role
sections.body: As lead full-stack engineer I own the architecture across the stack — designing the REST API surface, structuring the React/TypeScript frontend, and setting up the deployment and CI/CD workflow that ships changes to production safely.

sections.heading: Architecture
sections.body: The backend is organised as modular Django apps exposing versioned REST APIs, consumed by a single-page React/TypeScript client. That separation keeps the API reusable across surfaces (web and, later, mobile) and lets the frontend evolve independently of backend internals.

sections.heading: Deployment & Reliability
sections.body: The platform runs on secured Linux servers with an automated CI/CD pipeline, so releases are repeatable and low-risk. The emphasis is on high availability and a streamlined path from commit to production.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Shipped and maintained in production with high availability and a streamlined release pipeline.

projects.title: Feriaau CRM
projects.description: Multi-tenant CRM platform powering Feriaau's operations - robust REST APIs, optimized PostgreSQL/MySQL schemas, and strict tenant isolation across the data layer.
projects.outcome: Maximized application speed and secure multi-tenant isolation for concurrent organizations.

caseStudy.tagline: A multi-tenant CRM platform with strict tenant isolation across the data layer.
highlights: Multi-tenant architecture with strict per-tenant data isolation
Optimised PostgreSQL / MySQL schemas
Robust REST APIs powering concurrent organizations

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The Feriaau CRM is a multi-tenant SaaS platform that powers the operations side of Feriaau. The engineering centre of gravity is the data layer: robust REST APIs over optimised relational schemas, with strict isolation so multiple organizations share the same deployment without ever sharing data.

sections.heading: My Role
sections.body: I worked on the backend and data layer — designing the REST APIs, modelling and optimising the relational schemas, and implementing the tenant-isolation strategy.

sections.heading: Multi-Tenancy & Isolation
sections.body: The core challenge of any CRM is keeping each tenant's data provably separate while running on shared infrastructure. I implemented isolation across the data layer so every query is scoped to its tenant, preventing cross-tenant leakage by construction.

sections.heading: Data Layer
sections.body: Schemas were designed and indexed around the access patterns the CRM relies on, and tuned across PostgreSQL and MySQL to keep queries fast as data grows per organization.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Maximised application speed and secure multi-tenant isolation for concurrent organizations.

projects.title: Feriaau App
projects.description: Contributor to the Feriaau mobile app - a cross-platform Flutter application for Feriaau users, sharing backend services and REST APIs with the web platform.
projects.outcome: Published on the Google Play Store and Apple App Store for iOS and Android.

caseStudy.tagline: A cross-platform Flutter app sharing backend services with the Feriaau web platform.
highlights: Published on Google Play and the Apple App Store
Single cross-platform Flutter codebase
Shares REST APIs with the web platform

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The Feriaau app brings the platform to mobile as a single cross-platform Flutter codebase for iOS and Android. It reuses the same backend services and REST APIs as the web platform, so mobile and web stay consistent without duplicating the backend.

sections.heading: My Role
sections.body: I contributed to the Flutter application — building UI and wiring it to the shared REST APIs.

sections.heading: Cross-Platform Approach
sections.body: Using Flutter meant one Dart codebase targeting both iOS and Android, reusing the platform's existing REST API layer rather than standing up a separate mobile backend.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Published on the Google Play Store and Apple App Store for iOS and Android.

projects.title: ContractorOps AI App
projects.description: High-performance mobile application built with React Native and TypeScript for Contractor Ops AI, with refactored low-latency backend REST APIs and strict component state-management practices.
projects.outcome: Currently building the app: streamlining real-time application features and resolving full-stack integration bottlenecks across the stack.

caseStudy.tagline: A high-performance React Native app with a refactored low-latency REST backend.
highlights: React Native + TypeScript mobile app
Refactored low-latency backend REST APIs
Strict component state-management practices

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: ContractorOps AI is a mobile application I'm building with React Native and TypeScript. Alongside the app I refactored the backend REST APIs for lower latency, with a focus on real-time features and clean state management on the client.

sections.heading: My Role
sections.body: I'm building the mobile app and refactoring the supporting REST APIs — tightening latency and resolving integration bottlenecks across the stack.

sections.heading: Engineering Focus
sections.body: The client is written in React Native and TypeScript with strict, predictable state management. On the backend I refactored the REST APIs to cut latency, which is what makes the app's real-time features feel responsive.

sections.heading: Status
sections.body: Currently in active development — streamlining real-time features and resolving full-stack integration bottlenecks.

projects.title: ANPR + Smart Parking System
projects.description: Three-stage cascaded computer-vision pipeline (YOLOv8n → YOLOv11n → RT-DETR) for bi-scriptual Nepali plate recognition, serving as the backbone of an automated smart-parking platform.
projects.outcome: 90.4% end-to-end precision with 61%–73% compute reduction; powers automated billing and real-time traffic analytics.

caseStudy.tagline: A cascaded computer-vision pipeline for bi-scriptual Nepali plate recognition.
highlights: 90.4% end-to-end precision
61%–73% compute reduction
Three-stage cascaded detection pipeline

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: This project is a three-stage cascaded computer-vision pipeline (YOLOv8n → YOLOv11n → RT-DETR) built to recognise bi-scriptual Nepali number plates, and it serves as the backbone of an automated smart-parking platform.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.body: Nepali plates mix scripts and appear in messy real-world conditions, which makes single-model recognition brittle. The goal was recognition that's accurate but also cheap enough to run live inside a parking system.

sections.heading: Approach
sections.body: Rather than one heavy model, I cascaded three: a light YOLOv8n stage to localise, a YOLOv11n stage to refine, and RT-DETR for the hardest recognition step. Each stage only does the work the previous one couldn't — which is where the compute savings come from.

sections.heading: Results
sections.body: The pipeline reaches 90.4% end-to-end precision while cutting compute by 61%–73% versus a monolithic approach, and it powers automated billing and real-time traffic analytics in the smart-parking platform.

sections.heading: Try It
sections.body: The full detection workflow is runnable through the linked Roboflow workflow, and the source is on GitHub.

projects.title: Reddit Sentiment Analysis
projects.description: Deep NLP pipeline using fine-tuned BERT for nuanced emotion classification on large-scale Reddit data, integrated with LLM-based frontend interfaces.
projects.outcome: Nuanced multi-class emotion classification with advanced prompt-engineering integration.

caseStudy.tagline: A fine-tuned BERT pipeline for nuanced emotion classification on Reddit at scale.
highlights: Fine-tuned BERT for multi-class emotion classification
Large-scale Reddit dataset
LLM-integrated frontend interfaces

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: A deep NLP pipeline that uses a fine-tuned BERT model for nuanced, multi-class emotion classification on large-scale Reddit data, wired up to LLM-based frontend interfaces.

sections.heading: Approach
sections.body: I fine-tuned BERT on Reddit data to move past coarse positive/negative sentiment toward nuanced emotion classes. The trained model is exposed through an interface layer that pairs it with LLM prompt-engineering for richer interaction.

sections.heading: Engineering
sections.body: The pipeline covers the full path — large-scale data preparation, fine-tuning, and serving — so the emotion classifier can be queried directly from the frontend.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Nuanced multi-class emotion classification with advanced prompt-engineering integration.

projects.title: easy-3dkit
projects.description: Creator and maintainer of an open-source library of interactive 3D components for React, built natively on Three.js, React Three Fiber (R3F), and GSAP.
projects.outcome: Drop-in scroll-reactive surfaces, particle fields, instanced layouts, and post-processing - lightweight and production-ready.

caseStudy.tagline: An open-source library of drop-in interactive 3D components for React.
highlights: Published open-source on npm with live docs
Built natively on Three.js, R3F and GSAP
Scroll-reactive surfaces, particle fields, instanced layouts, post-processing

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: easy-3dkit is an open-source library of interactive 3D components for React that I created and maintain. It's built natively on Three.js, React Three Fiber (R3F) and GSAP, and gives developers drop-in 3D surfaces without wiring up the boilerplate themselves.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.body: Adding real-time 3D to a React app usually means a lot of Three.js/R3F setup before anything appears on screen. I wanted components you could drop in and configure — not a scene graph you have to hand-build every time.

sections.heading: What's Inside
sections.body: The kit ships scroll-reactive surfaces, particle fields, instanced layouts and post-processing effects — each a configurable React component that stays lightweight and production-ready.

sections.heading: Real-World Use
sections.body: It powers the live backdrop behind this very portfolio's terminal mode, which doubles as a proving ground for the library.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Drop-in scroll-reactive surfaces, particle fields, instanced layouts, and post-processing — lightweight and production-ready, published to npm with live docs.

projects.title: Partweave
projects.description: Creator of an open-source full-stack project scaffolder - a TypeScript CLI that generates self-contained monorepos from only the components you pick (Django, Next.js, Expo, plus auth, database, Docker and CI/CD modules), resolving dependencies and rejecting incompatible selections upfront.
projects.outcome: 'create-t3-app meets shadcn/ui' - deterministic template injection produces fully-owned, ready-to-run code; published to npm and extensible through a modules catalog.

caseStudy.tagline: A TypeScript CLI that scaffolds self-contained monorepos from only the parts you pick.
highlights: "create-t3-app meets shadcn/ui"
Deterministic template injection — you fully own the code
Dependency resolution with upfront incompatibility checks

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Partweave is an open-source full-stack project scaffolder — a TypeScript CLI that generates a self-contained monorepo from only the components you choose (Django, Next.js, Expo, plus auth, database, Docker and CI/CD modules). I describe it as 'create-t3-app meets shadcn/ui'.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.body: Starters either give you everything (and you delete half of it) or a fixed stack you can't deviate from. I wanted a scaffolder where you pick exactly the parts you need, get real code you fully own, and never fight incompatible selections after the fact.

sections.heading: Approach
sections.body: Partweave uses deterministic template injection: instead of pulling in a runtime dependency, it writes fully-owned, ready-to-run code into your repo. A dependency resolver validates your selections upfront and rejects incompatible combinations before it generates anything.

sections.heading: Architecture
sections.body: The generator is built around an extensible modules catalog — each module (a framework, auth, database, Docker, CI/CD) declares what it provides and what it needs, and the resolver weaves the compatible set into one coherent monorepo.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Deterministic template injection produces fully-owned, ready-to-run code; published to npm and extensible through a modules catalog.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio
projects.description: Designer and builder of my personal portfolio - a React/Vite site with Framer Motion motion design, a live Three.js/R3F backdrop powered by my own easy-3dkit, and an installable PWA. Ships a fully interactive terminal mode with a virtual filesystem and command parser as an alternate way to explore the site.
projects.outcome: Fast, installable, and playful - the site you're on right now, with a hidden shell for the curious.

caseStudy.tagline: The site you're on — React, live 3D, an installable PWA, and a hidden terminal.
highlights: Installable PWA with a fast, lazy-loaded critical path
Three distinct modes: interactive, CV, and terminal
Live 3D backdrop powered by my own easy-3dkit

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: This portfolio is a React/Vite site I designed and built from scratch. It offers three ways to explore the same content: an interactive motion-designed portfolio, a clean CV view, and a fully interactive terminal — plus a live Three.js backdrop and an offline-capable PWA.

sections.heading: Three Modes
sections.body: Visitors choose how they browse. 'Normal' is a Framer Motion interactive portfolio, 'CV' is a clean printable resume view, and 'Terminal' is a real shell with a virtual filesystem and command parser — all reading from a single content source of truth.

sections.heading: The Terminal
sections.body: The terminal mode implements a virtual filesystem and command parser, so the same project and experience data can be explored via commands. It even carousels live easy-3dkit effects behind the prompt.

sections.heading: Performance
sections.body: Each mode is its own lazy-loaded bundle, and heavy dependencies (framer-motion, three.js) are kept off the landing page's critical path so the mode selector loads fast. The site is an installable PWA that works offline.

sections.heading: Under the Hood
sections.body: The live 3D backdrop is driven by easy-3dkit — my own open-source library — which makes this site both a portfolio and a live demo of that work.

sections.heading: Outcome
sections.body: Fast, installable, and playful — the site you're on right now, with a hidden shell for the curious.
